name: S&P 500
summary: Down 6.77% from January peak. Four consecutive weekly losses. Capital rotating from tech into defense and energy stocks. Fed can't cut rates due to oil-driven inflation. Market in correction territory.

signals.content: Energy and defense stocks surged: Lockheed +15%, Exxon +5%+, Northrop +6%. Investors aggressively rotating from tech/growth into defense and energy.
S&P 500 dropped 1.5%, Nasdaq 100 plunged 1.8% to six-month lows. Correction territory. Stocks, bonds, and gold all selling off simultaneously.

S&P 500 soared 2.2% (+143 pts) to 6,605 after Trump announced 5-day strike pause. Major relief rally. US markets reacted to de-escalation signal; Asian markets had already sold off on the earlier escalation threats (time-zone divergence).

edges.context: Primary US equity benchmark. Reflecting war costs, inflation fears, and Fed uncertainty.
Fed can't cut → no valuation support. Market pricing possible hike → higher discount rates → compression.
Oil spike → cost inflation → margin compression for most sectors. Energy sector benefits but tech-heavy index suffers net.
Helium shortage from Qatar → semiconductor supply risk → AI infrastructure uncertainty → tech valuation pressure. Novel risk.

trigger_points.condition: S&P 500 enters bear market (down 20% from peak)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would force Fed to consider emergency support despite inflation. Would crash global markets including India. Could paradoxically lead to war de-escalation pressure.
